L1.area: CGI and FastCGI request data processing and protocol handling

L2.description: An uninitialized memory buffer is allocated and then unsafely marked as fully populated without zeroing its contents. This buffer is subsequently provided to a user-supplied reader implementation, which may read from the uninitialized memory region, leading to undefined behavior and potential information disclosure.

L3.area: FastCGI protocol record parsing and key-value pair iteration
L3.description: During FastCGI key-value pair iteration, uninitialized memory buffers are allocated and then unsafely marked as fully populated without zeroing their contents. These buffers are subsequently passed to a user-supplied reader implementation, which may read from the uninitialized memory, leading to undefined behavior and potential information disclosure.

ground_truth.reason: The Iterator implementation for KeyValueReader creates uninitialized memory buffers using Vec::with_capacity followed by unsafe set_len, then passes them to a user-provided Read implementation, exposing uninitialized heap memory to safe Rust code.
